volatility: SettlementWorkflow volatility: the sequence by which the platform onboards payment integration, ingests inbound revenue and reversals, closes a billing cycle (read→compute→route→record), recomputes on chargeback, and sweeps shortfalls into a delinquency signal.

inbound.name: merchantWebhookClient
inbound.how: sync in-process (SignalWorkflow); webhook signature verified upstream by Client via security Utility

outbound.name: settlementEngine
outbound.how: direct by value in-workflow (NOT an Activity); pure/deterministic

outbound.name: interventionEngine
outbound.how: direct by value in-workflow (NOT an Activity); pure/deterministic

outbound.name: revenueLedgerAccess
outbound.how: Activity-wrapped; writes dedup on gateway event id

outbound.name: durableExecutionAccess
outbound.how: Activity-wrapped for category-B verbs (deliverSignal, registerSchedule); awaitSignal is category-A in-workflow primitive (NOT an op)

outbound.name: operationsManager
outbound.how: queued M→M via durableExecutionAccess.deliverSignal (NOT a direct call); the one sanctioned queued M→M edge

ops.note: Resolves settlement aggregate from deployedAppId, creates merchant connected account, wires payment config into runtime, records binding, registers per-customer closeSettlementCycle Schedule. Idempotent on workflow id {customerId}:onboard.

fields.name: DeployedAppId
fields.note: operated-app identity; resolved to CustomerID inside the workflow via settlementStateAccess.readSettlement

fields.name: CustomerID
fields.note: canonical settlement aggregate identity — continuity token

ops.note: Validates stored payment instrument (zero-amount auth) and opens the settlement aggregate. Idempotent on workflow id {customerId}:register.

fields.name: CustomerId
fields.note: canonical settlement aggregate identity (CustomerID = uuid.UUID per D-MST §3.0)

fields.name: CustomerID
fields.note: continuity token for the registered aggregate

ops.note: Reads revenue + usage ranges, computes signed net in-workflow by value (settlementEngine.computeNet), executes routing directive (payout/charge), on failure calls interventionEngine, records outcome. Stays alive awaiting inbound/reversal/chargeback signals. Workflow id {customerId}:{cycleId}:close is the continuity token.
Reads persistently-delinquent customers and delivers queued applyDelinquencyPolicy Signal to operationsManager via durableExecutionAccess.deliverSignal for each. Does not pause/withdraw apps itself.

SignalWorkflow to cycle workflow {customerId}:{cycleId}:close (signal inboundRevenueReceived); signal-with-start if cycle not yet running. Appends revenue fact to Revenue Ledger, idempotent on gateway event id.

SignalWorkflow to cycle workflow (signal chargebackReceived); signal-with-start → recomputeCycle if original close completed. Appends reversal, recomputes net forward-only, records correction, routes delta. Compensation is forward-only; no rollback.

fields.name: ReversesId
fields.note: optional; gateway event id of the original revenue event

dataContracts: CustomerID = uuid.UUID  // canonical settlement aggregate identity key — ruled at D-MST §3.0
CycleID = string        // agreed across D-RA / D-UA / settlementStateAccess
DeployedAppID = uuid.UUID // operated-app identity; NOT the settlement key
CloseCycleResult struct { CustomerID CustomerID; CycleID CycleID; Routed RoutingDirective }
GatewayRevenueEvent struct { /* gateway event id, customer/cycle, signed amount+currency, occurred-at */ }
GatewayReversalEvent struct { /* gateway event id, affected cycle, reversed amount+currency, reverses-id? */ }
errorModel: SettlementError is framework-go/manager.Error with kinds: FailedPrecondition / NotFound / Conflict / Unauthorized / Transient / ContractMisuse. These are caller/programmer errors at the façade boundary — distinct from the workflow's own failure handling (Temporal RetryPolicy + interventionEngine decide→execute + forward-only chargeback compensation).
idempotency: Workflow-level: idempotent on workflow id (a redundant StartWorkflow returns the running SettlementRef). Activity-level: each RA write Activity carries Manager-derived idempotencyKey and expectedVersion; writes use dedup-first ordering. Gateway charges use Stripe Idempotency-Key = settle:{customerId}:{cycleId}. Signal ops idempotent on gateway event id (D-RA §2).

revisions.summary: First draft: 6 ops (4 Workflow + 2 Signal), canonical settlement aggregate identity ruled as CustomerID=uuid.UUID (closing D-RA OQ-3 and D-UA §9 Q3), decides FU-MST-1 (settlementStateAccess id-type migration) and FU-MST-2 (merchantGatewayAccess D-MA contract).

APPROVED-AND-FROZEN. Independent re-derivation confirmed 3 Clients, 6 ops, outbound strictly down one layer. Canonical-key ruling RATIFIED. OQ-4 clarified: Escalate = operator-dashboard via settlementStateAccess head-state, no new edge. All OQs ruled; follow-ups FU-MST-1 through FU-MST-4 routed.
